meanings.definition: A person who flatters powerful people in a servile way to gain advantage.
examples: The king was surrounded by sycophants who never told him the truth.
She refused to become a sycophant just to win a promotion.

meanings.definition: To follow a winding, turning course rather than a straight path.
examples: The river meanders through the green valley.
We meandered along the lane with no particular destination.

meanings.definition: To wander or proceed in an aimless, unhurried way.
examples: The lecture meandered from one topic to another.

meanings.definition: The science that deals with matter, energy, and the forces and laws governing them.
examples: She majored in physics at university.
The laws of physics govern how the rocket moves.

meanings.definition: A place or store where things are collected and kept.
examples: The archive served as a repertory of the nation's records.
His mind was a repertory of facts and anecdotes.

meanings.definition: The set of plays or pieces a company is prepared to perform.
examples: The theater added three new works to its repertory this season.

meanings.definition: Customary, usual, or conventional.
examples: The dictionary records the nomic spelling of the word.
Scholars debated whether the usage was nomic or merely poetic.

meanings.definition: A musician who plays a supporting part alongside a soloist or main performer.
examples: The singer thanked her piano accompanist after the recital.
A skilled accompanist can follow a soloist's every change of pace.

meanings.definition: To push or force something out.
examples: The machine extrudes molten plastic into long strips.
Toothpaste is extruded from the tube when you squeeze it.

meanings.definition: A very poor person with no means of supporting themselves.
examples: The once-wealthy merchant died a pauper.
Charity houses once sheltered the town's paupers.

meanings.definition: Not costing much money; cheap.
examples: They found an inexpensive hotel near the station.
The meal was tasty and surprisingly inexpensive.

meanings.definition: The supposed transfer of thoughts or feelings from one mind to another without using the senses.
examples: The twins joked that they communicated by telepathy.
Scientists have found no reliable evidence for telepathy.

meanings.definition: Affecting a whole people or region, as a disease that spreads widely.
examples: The pandemic flu swept across every continent within months.

meanings.definition: An outbreak of a disease that spreads across a large region or the entire world.
examples: Schools closed for months during the pandemic.

meanings.definition: Swollen or distended, as with fluid.
examples: The injured ankle was turgid and painful to touch.

meanings.definition: Excessively elaborate or pompous in language or style.
examples: The report was so turgid that few people finished reading it.

meanings.definition: Innate or existing as a natural part of someone from birth.
examples: She had an inbred sense of fairness that guided every decision.

meanings.definition: Produced by breeding closely related individuals.
examples: The inbred line of dogs suffered from many genetic problems.

meanings.definition: The condition of being more important and so coming or being dealt with first.
examples: Safety takes precedence over speed on every project.

meanings.definition: The order of rank or position held by people of high status.
examples: At the ceremony, guests were seated according to precedence.

meanings.definition: Not clearly seen, heard, or defined; vague.
examples: Through the fog, the buildings were only indistinct shapes.

meanings.definition: A fixed rule or set form, often expressed in symbols, for doing or calculating something.
examples: The students memorized the formula for the area of a circle.

meanings.definition: A prepared liquid food for feeding infants in place of breast milk.
examples: The baby was switched to formula at three months.

meanings.definition: Showing a lack of respect, especially toward sacred or serious things.
examples: His irreverent jokes during the service offended several guests.

meanings.definition: To settle or arrange something beforehand.
examples: They preestablished the rules before the game began.

meanings.definition: Less important or central than something else; coming after the primary thing.
examples: For her, money was secondary to doing work she loved.

meanings.definition: Relating to education between primary school and college or university.
examples: He teaches history at a secondary school.

meanings.definition: Capable of being cured, remedied, or corrected.
examples: Doctors confirmed that the infection was easily curable with antibiotics.

meanings.definition: To rub out or wipe away completely; obliterate.
examples: Years of wind and rain had effaced the inscription on the stone.

meanings.definition: To make oneself inconspicuous or modestly withdraw attention.
examples: She tried to efface herself at the back of the room.

meanings.definition: To formally promise or engage someone to be married.
examples: The young prince was betrothed to a princess from a neighboring kingdom.

meanings.definition: Something that silences, suppresses, or brings an end, especially a final one.
examples: The court ruling gave the long dispute its quietus.

meanings.definition: Disagreement with or opposition to a prevailing opinion or official decision.
examples: The new policy was passed despite loud dissent from the staff.

meanings.definition: To hold or express an opinion that differs from the official or majority view.
examples: Two judges dissented from the court's ruling.

meanings.definition: To catch sight of something, especially at a distance or with difficulty.
examples: From the hilltop she espied a thin trail of smoke in the valley.

meanings.definition: Not needed or essential under the circumstances.
examples: He removed all the unnecessary words to make the essay shorter.

meanings.definition: In, at, or to whatever place.
examples: You can sit wherever you like.

meanings.definition: Used to express surprise about where something is or happened.
examples: Wherever did you find such an old photograph?

meanings.definition: Relating to the body or the physical senses, especially as a source of pleasure.
examples: The poem described the sensual delights of a summer garden.

meanings.definition: Not wise or sensible to do; not recommended.
examples: It is inadvisable to hike alone in such steep terrain.

meanings.definition: Lying flat on the back, with the face turned upward.
examples: The patient lay supine on the table while the nurse took an X-ray.

meanings.definition: Failing to act or resist out of laziness or moral weakness.
examples: The citizens were supine in the face of growing corruption.

meanings.definition: To put things in a definite, proper, or pleasing order.
examples: She arranged the books on the shelf by color.

meanings.definition: To plan or organize something in advance.
examples: We arranged to meet at the station at noon.

meanings.definition: To give official approval or permission for something.
examples: The board sanctioned the new building project.

meanings.definition: A penalty or coercive measure imposed to enforce a rule or law.
examples: The country faced trade sanctions for breaking the agreement.

meanings.definition: Active opposition or hostility between people or forces.
examples: There was long-standing antagonism between the two rival clubs.

meanings.definition: Kept secret or hidden, often for an improper purpose.
examples: The agents carried out a covert operation behind enemy lines.

meanings.definition: Conformity to the truth; truthfulness.
examples: No one doubted the veracity of the witness's account.

meanings.definition: Composed of or resembling small grains or particles.
examples: The recipe calls for granular sugar rather than powdered.

meanings.definition: Showing a high level of fine detail.
examples: The report gave a granular breakdown of every expense.

meanings.definition: Not showing or done with proper care for the consequences; careless of one's duties.
examples: It was irresponsible to leave the children unsupervised near the pool.

meanings.definition: The quality of recurring or happening at regular intervals.
examples: Scientists studied the periodicity of the comet's return.

meanings.definition: Covered with hair; hairy.
examples: His hirsute arms were visible beneath his rolled-up sleeves.

meanings.definition: Showing a lack of respect, especially toward sacred or serious things.
examples: The crowd's irreverential laughter shocked the elderly priest.

meanings.definition: Using dishonest or underhanded methods to achieve a goal.
examples: He used devious tricks to win the contract.

meanings.definition: Departing from the direct or usual course; roundabout.
examples: They took a devious route through back roads to avoid the traffic.

meanings.definition: A loud, resonant, clanging or ringing noise, as of bells, arms, or chains.
examples: The clangor of the church bells filled the entire town.

meanings.definition: Resembling or characteristic of a man.
examples: The robot moved with strange, manlike gestures.

meanings.definition: A rhythmical or measured flow of sound or movement.
examples: The marchers stepped in time to the steady cadence of the drum.

meanings.definition: The rising and falling pattern of a person's voice.
examples: She spoke with a gentle, soothing cadence.

meanings.definition: A painted or sculptured image of the Virgin Mary, often with the infant Jesus.
examples: The chapel was famous for its medieval Madonna carved in oak.

meanings.definition: A place or building where dead bodies are cremated.
examples: The funeral procession made its way to the crematory.

meanings.definition: Relating to the cremation of dead bodies.
examples: The crematory furnace reaches extremely high temperatures.

meanings.definition: Able to express ideas or feelings fluently and movingly in speech or writing.
examples: Her eloquent speech brought the whole audience to tears.

meanings.definition: Of or relating to numbers.
examples: The data was sorted in numerical order.

meanings.definition: To settle a dispute by acting as an impartial judge between the parties.
examples: A neutral panel was asked to arbitrate the labor dispute.

meanings.definition: Falling off or shed at a particular stage of growth or season, such as leaves or petals.
examples: Oaks and maples are deciduous trees that lose their leaves in autumn.

meanings.definition: To like or value one thing more than another.
examples: I prefer tea to coffee in the mornings.

meanings.definition: A false name used in place of one's real name, especially by a writer.
examples: The author published her first novel under a pseudonym.

meanings.definition: A person who held a position or office before someone else.
examples: The new mayor reversed many policies of her predecessor.

meanings.definition: A thing that came before another of the same kind.
examples: This phone is much faster than its predecessor.

meanings.definition: Occurring at or marked by equal intervals of time.
examples: A pendulum's swings are isochronous, taking the same time regardless of size.

meanings.definition: A group, especially of military officers, that rules a country after seizing power by force.
examples: The military junta suspended the constitution and arrested opposition leaders.

meanings.definition: To cut off a limb or other part of the body, usually by surgery.
examples: Surgeons had to amputate the soldier's leg below the knee.

meanings.definition: A three-wheeled vehicle, typically pedaled by a young child.
examples: The toddler rode his red tricycle around the yard.

meanings.definition: Conforming to accepted standards of proper conduct, speech, or appearance.
examples: Please put on some decent clothes before our guests arrive.

meanings.definition: Of an acceptable or satisfactory standard.
examples: She earns a decent salary at her new job.

meanings.definition: To father a child; to produce offspring.
examples: In the old story, the king begot seven sons.

meanings.definition: To cause or give rise to something.
examples: Violence often begets more violence.

meanings.definition: To put a date on a document or check that is later than the actual date.
examples: He postdated the check so it could not be cashed until next week.

meanings.definition: To occur or come later in time than something else.
examples: These ruins postdate the earlier settlement by several centuries.

meanings.definition: To dishonestly deprive someone of money, rights, or property.
examples: He was jailed for trying to defraud investors of millions.

meanings.definition: A position of high rank, distinction, or importance.
examples: She rose to eminence as a leading heart surgeon.

meanings.definition: A piece of rising ground; a hill or elevation.
examples: The castle stood on a grassy eminence above the river.

meanings.definition: To spread or scatter something, especially information, widely.
examples: The agency uses social media to disseminate weather warnings.

meanings.definition: Highly competent and skilled in an art, science, or activity.
examples: She is proficient in three languages.

meanings.definition: Very careful and conscientious, taking pains to do what is right.
examples: The accountant kept scrupulous records of every transaction.

meanings.definition: Diligent and thorough in attention to detail.
examples: The restoration required scrupulous attention to the original colors.

meanings.definition: A mass departure of people from a place.
examples: The factory's closure caused an exodus from the small town.

meanings.definition: The branch of physics dealing with the equilibrium and mechanical properties of gases, especially air.
examples: Aerostatics explains why a hot-air balloon rises.

meanings.definition: A wooden framework with holes for the head and hands, once used to publicly punish and shame offenders.
examples: In the old town square, criminals were once locked in the pillory.

meanings.definition: To attack or ridicule someone publicly.
examples: The newspapers pilloried the politician for his broken promises.

meanings.definition: The troops stationed in a fort or town to defend it.
examples: A small garrison held the fortress through the long siege.

meanings.definition: To station troops in a place to defend it.
examples: The general decided to garrison the captured city.

meanings.definition: To get something back, especially by searching for it.
examples: She ran upstairs to retrieve her forgotten keys.

meanings.definition: To find and bring back stored information.
examples: The program can retrieve any file in seconds.

meanings.definition: To raise or mention a subject for the first time, especially a difficult one.
examples: He finally broached the topic of moving to another city.

meanings.definition: A private meeting of members of a political party to choose candidates or decide policy.
examples: The party held a caucus to pick its nominee for governor.

meanings.definition: A short story that illustrates a moral or spiritual lesson.
examples: The teacher told a parable about kindness to strangers.

meanings.definition: To twist something out of its natural or normal shape.
examples: The heat had distorted the plastic frame.

meanings.definition: To give a misleading or false account of something.
examples: The article distorted her words to make her sound rude.

meanings.definition: To regard something as likely to happen or to come.
examples: We expect heavy rain later this afternoon.

meanings.definition: To require something as proper or due.
examples: The teacher expects her students to arrive on time.

meanings.definition: Round, fat, or swollen in shape, like a bulb.
examples: The cartoon character had a big, bulbous nose.

meanings.definition: Used to express possibility or uncertainty; maybe.
examples: Perhaps we should leave before the storm arrives.

meanings.definition: To weigh down or hold back with a burden or obstacle.
examples: Heavy backpacks encumbered the hikers on the steep climb.

meanings.definition: To think deeply and at length about something.
examples: She ruminated for days over whether to accept the offer.

meanings.definition: To chew partly digested food again, as cattle do.
examples: The cows lay in the shade, ruminating slowly.

meanings.definition: Able to float or to keep something afloat.
examples: The life jacket is buoyant enough to support an adult.

meanings.definition: Cheerful and full of energy or optimism.
examples: She was in a buoyant mood after passing her exams.

meanings.definition: Tending to cause or bring on sleep.
examples: The somniferous drone of the lecturer soon had half the class nodding off.
Warm milk is often regarded as a somniferous bedtime drink.

meanings.definition: Free from prejudice or favoritism; impartial in judgment.
examples: A juror must remain unbiased and weigh only the evidence presented.
She gave an unbiased review of both candidates' proposals.

meanings.definition: Causing annoyance, weariness, or irritation; tiresome.
examples: Filing the same forms every week is an irksome chore.
His constant interruptions grew increasingly irksome.

meanings.definition: To make amends or reparation for a wrong or offense.
examples: He spent years trying to atone for the mistakes of his youth.
She volunteered at the shelter to atone for her earlier neglect.

meanings.definition: Showing little or no emotion or sensitivity; impassive.
examples: The guard remained stolid even as the crowd shouted at him.
Her stolid expression gave no hint of the shock she felt.

meanings.definition: A secret plot or scheme intended to achieve something by underhanded means.
examples: The palace was a hotbed of political intrigue.
The novel is full of intrigue, betrayal, and hidden alliances.

meanings.definition: The quality of arousing curiosity or fascination.
examples: There was an air of intrigue about the abandoned mansion.

meanings.definition: To arouse the curiosity or interest of someone.
examples: The mysterious title intrigued her enough to open the book.

meanings.definition: To set apart or separate something from others of its kind.
examples: Scientists worked to isolate the gene responsible for the disorder.
Nurses isolate patients with contagious illnesses to prevent spread.

meanings.definition: Dependent on or conditioned by something else; not certain in advance.
examples: Funding for the project is contingent on board approval.
Their travel plans were contingent upon the weather.

meanings.definition: The middle period of summer, around the summer solstice.
examples: By midsummer the garden was bursting with ripe tomatoes.
They held the festival every midsummer in the village square.

meanings.definition: Spreading or flourishing unchecked; widespread and uncontrolled.
examples: Corruption was rampant throughout the failing regime.
Weeds grew rampant across the neglected field.

meanings.definition: Too great or intense to be put into words; indescribable.
examples: She felt an inexpressible joy when she saw her family again.
The beauty of the sunset was almost inexpressible.

meanings.definition: Lacking ambition, energy, or resourcefulness; lazy.
examples: His shiftless habits cost him one job after another.
The town blamed its decline on a few shiftless landlords.

meanings.definition: A statement or proposition taken as the basis for an argument or conclusion.
examples: The whole theory rests on a single flawed premise.
If the premise is false, the conclusion cannot be trusted.

meanings.definition: A building together with its land and outbuildings, considered as a property (usually plural, premises).
examples: No smoking is allowed on the premises.

meanings.definition: Unreasonably or unjustifiably excessive; not guided by conscience.
examples: The lender charged an unconscionable rate of interest.
It would be unconscionable to ignore their suffering.

meanings.definition: A severe or intensive questioning or investigation, historically a tribunal for examining and punishing heretics.
examples: The medieval Inquisition tried thousands on charges of heresy.
The interview felt less like a chat and more like an inquisition.

meanings.definition: Able to be grasped or understood by the mind.
examples: Some abstract ideas are scarcely apprehensible to a beginner.
He explained the theory in terms apprehensible to anyone.

meanings.definition: To identify someone or something seen or known before.
examples: I barely recognized her after all these years.
He recognized the tune within the first few notes.

meanings.definition: To acknowledge the existence, validity, or importance of something.
examples: The committee recognized her years of dedicated service.

meanings.definition: Great generosity in giving.
examples: The library was built thanks to the munificence of a single donor.
Guests marveled at the host's munificence.

meanings.definition: The state or time of being an unmarried young woman or virgin.
examples: In the old tale she guarded her maidenhood until marriage.
The poem mourns the fleeting days of her maidenhood.

meanings.definition: To act on or have an effect upon one another.
examples: The children learned to interact politely with their classmates.
Researchers study how the two chemicals interact.

meanings.definition: A vague or unspecific statement that lacks detail.
examples: The speech was full of generalities and offered no real plan.
Avoid generalities and give us concrete examples.

meanings.definition: The greater part or majority of something.
examples: The generality of voters favored the reform.

meanings.definition: A small place or community regarded as a miniature representation of a larger whole.
examples: The crowded market was a microcosm of the entire city.
The classroom served as a microcosm of society at large.

meanings.definition: To cause oneself to recall or consider; to remind oneself.
examples: She paused to bethink herself of the proper words.
He bethought himself of an old promise he had made.

meanings.definition: Relating to or resembling the moon.
examples: The astronauts collected samples of lunar rock.
Many calendars are based on the lunar cycle.

meanings.definition: Not able to be changed or persuaded; rigid in attitude or form.
examples: The rules were inflexible, allowing for no exceptions.
His inflexible schedule left no room for spontaneity.

meanings.definition: Existing or produced in great or abundant amounts.
examples: She offered profuse apologies for the delay.
The patient was sweating profusely with a profuse fever.

meanings.definition: A flat shape with six straight sides and six angles.
examples: A honeycomb is made up of countless tiny hexagons.
The students measured the angles of a regular hexagon.

meanings.definition: To form or cause to form into tight curls or crinkles, especially hair.
examples: The damp air made her bangs frizzle by midday.
She frizzled her hair with a curling iron before the party.

meanings.definition: Allowing light to pass through; clear and easy to understand.
examples: The pellucid water revealed the pebbles on the lake bottom.
His pellucid prose made the difficult topic easy to grasp.

meanings.definition: Not allowed or acceptable, especially as evidence in court.
examples: The judge ruled the confession inadmissible.
Hearsay is generally inadmissible as testimony.

meanings.definition: A published false statement that damages a person's reputation.
examples: The actor sued the tabloid for libel.
Printing those false claims could amount to libel.

meanings.definition: To publish a false and damaging statement about someone.
examples: He claimed the newspaper had libeled him.

meanings.definition: Allowing light to pass through so that objects behind can be clearly seen.
examples: The window was made of transparent glass.
She wrapped the gift in transparent plastic.

meanings.definition: Easy to perceive, understand, or detect; open and honest.
examples: His excuse was a transparent lie.
The company promised to be transparent about its finances.

meanings.definition: The process or cost of keeping something in good condition; maintenance.
examples: The upkeep of the old mansion was enormous.
Regular upkeep keeps the engine running smoothly.

meanings.definition: The point in the orbit of the moon or a satellite where it is nearest the earth.
examples: The moon appears largest when it reaches perigee.
At perigee the satellite skims closest to the planet.

meanings.definition: Able to be dissolved, especially in a liquid.
examples: Salt is highly soluble in water.
These vitamins are soluble and easily absorbed by the body.

meanings.definition: Capable of being solved or resolved.
examples: The puzzle proved soluble once we found the key.

meanings.definition: A central part or core around which other things are gathered.
examples: A few loyal members formed the nucleus of the new club.
The control-laden nucleus of the cell holds its genetic material.

meanings.definition: A general dislike or distrust of humankind.
examples: Years of betrayal hardened him into bitter misanthropy.
The hermit's misanthropy kept all visitors away.

meanings.definition: A small, narrow body of water that leads inland from a larger body of water.
examples: The fishing boats sheltered in a quiet inlet.
A narrow inlet connected the lagoon to the open sea.

meanings.definition: To turn away (one's eyes or thoughts).
examples: She averted her gaze from the gruesome scene.

meanings.definition: To prevent something undesirable from happening.
examples: Quick action by the pilot averted a disaster.

meanings.definition: So small as to be visible only with a microscope; extremely tiny.
examples: The water teemed with microscopic organisms.
He examined the microscopic cracks in the metal.

meanings.definition: Extremely dirty and unpleasant, often due to poverty or neglect.
examples: The refugees lived in squalid, overcrowded shelters.
They escaped the squalid tenement at last.

meanings.definition: A medical condition marked by yellowing of the skin and eyes, caused by excess bile pigment.
examples: The newborn was treated for jaundice under special lights.
His yellowed eyes were an early sign of jaundice.

meanings.definition: The ability to withstand hardship, pain, or prolonged effort without giving up.
examples: Marathon running demands tremendous endurance.
Her endurance through the illness amazed the doctors.

meanings.definition: To trim or cut away the outer surface or edges of something.
examples: She began to pare the apple with a small knife.
The company had to pare its budget to stay afloat.

meanings.definition: A group of living organisms classified below a genus, whose members can interbreed.
examples: The biologist discovered a new species of frog.
Many species are threatened by habitat loss.

meanings.definition: A kind, sort, or type of something.
examples: This is a peculiar species of argument.

meanings.definition: Bearing cones, as pines, firs, and other evergreen trees.
examples: Coniferous forests cover much of the northern landscape.
Pines and spruces are coniferous trees.

meanings.definition: A legal judgment that officially clears a person of a criminal charge.
examples: The jury's acquittal set the defendant free.
His lawyers celebrated the unexpected acquittal.

meanings.definition: Aggressively active in support of a cause; combative.
examples: The union took a militant stance against the layoffs.
Militant protesters clashed with police.

meanings.definition: A bright, fiercely burning flame or fire.
examples: Firefighters battled the blaze for hours.
The campfire grew into a roaring blaze.

meanings.definition: A bright display of light or color.
examples: The garden was a blaze of autumn color.

meanings.definition: Able to be made smaller, simpler, or brought to a different form.
examples: The fraction is reducible to its lowest terms.
Their complaints were reducible to a single grievance.

meanings.definition: Of a reddish-brown color, usually said of hair.
examples: Her auburn hair shone in the sunlight.
He had a thick auburn beard.

meanings.definition: To involve something as a necessary part or consequence.
examples: Running a business entails a great deal of risk.
The new job entails frequent travel.

meanings.definition: Displaying or marked by great wealth and luxury.
examples: They dined in an opulent hall hung with chandeliers.
The palace was decorated in opulent style.

meanings.definition: A shortage or insufficiency of something needed.
examples: A scarcity of clean water plagued the region.
The scarcity of jobs forced many to move away.

meanings.definition: An open and deliberate insult or act of disrespect.
examples: He took the remark as a personal affront.
Being overlooked for the award felt like an affront.

meanings.definition: To insult someone openly or to their face.
examples: She was affronted by his rude dismissal.

meanings.definition: To argue or dispute noisily and persistently.
examples: The committee wrangled for hours over the budget.
They wrangled about who should pay the bill.

meanings.definition: To round up and herd livestock, especially cattle or horses.
examples: The cowboys wrangled the horses into the corral.

meanings.definition: Aggressive defiance or fierce, combative ferocity.
examples: His truculence made every negotiation a battle.
She faced the bully's truculence with calm resolve.

meanings.definition: Careful good judgment that avoids unnecessary risks.
examples: With great prudence, she saved money for emergencies.
Prudence dictated that they wait for better weather.

meanings.definition: To lower someone's spirits; to dishearten.
examples: The repeated failures began to deject the whole team.
Nothing could deject her optimistic outlook.

meanings.definition: A person who travels from place to place preaching and seeking to convert others to a faith.
examples: The evangelist drew huge crowds to his tent revivals.
She became a tireless evangelist for the cause.

meanings.definition: Strongly and often blindly devoted to a particular party, cause, or person.
examples: The vote split along strictly partisan lines.
He grew tired of the partisan bickering in Congress.

meanings.definition: A firm supporter of a party, cause, or person.
examples: She was a lifelong partisan of the reform movement.

meanings.definition: Extremely wicked or cruel, as if belonging to the devil.
examples: The villain hatched a diabolic plan.
There was a diabolic cunning in his scheme.

meanings.definition: Easily broken, damaged, or destroyed; delicate.
examples: Handle the fragile vase with care.
The peace agreement remained fragile.

meanings.definition: The highest or topmost point of something.
examples: She reached the pinnacle of her career at thirty.
Snow crowned the rocky pinnacle of the peak.

meanings.definition: Extremely overweight; grossly fat.
examples: The doctor warned that he was dangerously obese.
An obese cat lounged on the windowsill.

meanings.definition: The branch of philosophy concerned with the fundamental nature of reality and existence.
examples: Her thesis explored questions of metaphysics and free will.
Metaphysics asks what it truly means for something to exist.

meanings.definition: A doctor who specializes in pregnancy, childbirth, and the care of mothers.
examples: The obstetrician monitored the baby's heartbeat.
She chose an experienced obstetrician for her first pregnancy.

meanings.definition: Extremely large; enormous.
examples: A gigantic wave crashed over the pier.
They faced a gigantic task in rebuilding the city.

meanings.definition: Very large, heavy, and solid in size or scale.
examples: A massive boulder blocked the road.
The company suffered massive losses last year.

meanings.definition: Becoming outdated and gradually passing out of use.
examples: Many slang terms are obsolescent within a decade.
The factory's obsolescent machinery could no longer compete.

meanings.definition: Keen, intuitive insight or perception that seems to go beyond ordinary reasoning.
examples: Her clairvoyance allowed her to sense a problem in the deal before anyone else noticed it.

meanings.definition: The supposed power to perceive things or events beyond normal sensory contact.
examples: The medium claimed that her clairvoyance let her see distant events as they happened.

meanings.definition: The branch of medicine concerned with the diagnosis and treatment of mental illness.
examples: After medical school she specialized in psychiatry to help patients with depression and anxiety.
Modern psychiatry combines medication with talk therapy.

meanings.definition: To combine several separate things into a single unified whole.
examples: The company consolidated its three regional offices into one headquarters.

meanings.definition: To make something stronger or more secure.
examples: She used the new evidence to consolidate her position in the debate.

meanings.definition: The act of talking about something in order to exchange ideas or reach a decision.
examples: The committee held a long discussion about the new budget.
After much discussion, they finally agreed on a plan.

meanings.definition: To confuse or make something unclear, as if covering it in fog.
examples: The lawyer's tangled questions seemed designed to befog the jury.
Too much jargon can befog an otherwise simple explanation.

meanings.definition: A soft, gentle breeze.
examples: A warm zephyr drifted through the open window on the summer evening.
The sails barely moved in the light zephyr.

meanings.definition: A wealthy and influential person, especially in business or industry.
examples: The oil magnate funded several universities and museums.
She became a media magnate after buying a string of newspapers.

meanings.definition: To stir up or urge someone to take a particular, often harmful, action.
examples: The speech was meant to incite the crowd to riot.
He was accused of inciting violence against the protesters.

meanings.definition: Giving a feeling of pleasure or enjoyment.
examples: A long walk on the beach is a pleasurable way to relax.
They spent a pleasurable afternoon reading in the garden.

meanings.definition: Cheerfully optimistic and confident about the future.
examples: She remained sanguine about her chances even after the first round of cuts.
He took a sanguine view of the economy despite the bad news.

meanings.definition: Having a healthy reddish or blood-red color.
examples: His sanguine complexion gave him a look of robust health.

meanings.definition: Slow to act or speak because of uncertainty or unwillingness.
examples: She was hesitant to share her opinion in front of the whole class.
He gave a hesitant nod before agreeing to the plan.

meanings.definition: Showing intense passion, enthusiasm, or devotion.
examples: He was an ardent supporter of the local team.
Her ardent love of music led her to study the violin for years.

meanings.definition: An unlucky accident or unfortunate event, usually a minor one.
examples: A small mishap in the kitchen left the cake slightly burned.
Despite a few mishaps, the trip went smoothly overall.

meanings.definition: Not alive; lacking the qualities of living things.
examples: A rock is an inanimate object, while a tree is alive.
The child spoke to her inanimate toys as if they could hear her.

meanings.definition: Relating to or used in directly addressing or calling a person or thing.
examples: In Latin, the vocative case is used when speaking directly to someone.
'O captain!' is a vocative expression that calls out to the listener.

meanings.definition: To pour out or give off, as a liquid, light, or feeling.
examples: The cracked pipe effused a thin stream of water.
Warmth seemed to effuse from her welcoming smile.

meanings.definition: To make or become four times as great or as many.
examples: The company managed to quadruple its profits in just two years.
If you quadruple two, you get eight.

meanings.definition: Consisting of four parts or four times as much.
examples: They booked a quadruple room for the four of them.

meanings.definition: To deceive or trick someone.
examples: The con artist hoodwinked several investors out of their savings.
Don't let those flashy ads hoodwink you into buying junk.

meanings.definition: Showing very strong and intense feeling.
examples: She gave a fervid speech in defense of free education.
His fervid enthusiasm for the cause inspired the whole team.

meanings.definition: A part that bulges or swells out from a surrounding surface.
examples: A small protuberance on the old door turned out to be a hidden latch.
The knotted protuberance on the tree trunk made a natural seat.

meanings.definition: Polite respect that leads one to yield to another's opinion or wishes.
examples: Out of deference to her experience, the younger staff followed her lead.
He spoke with deference to the elderly professor.

meanings.definition: Extremely dry, especially because of little or no rainfall.
examples: Few plants can survive in the arid desert.
The arid climate made farming nearly impossible.

meanings.definition: Lacking interest, warmth, or liveliness.
examples: The lecture was an arid recitation of dates and names.

meanings.definition: Extremely hungry or eager, as if starving.
examples: After the long hike, the children were ravenous and ate everything in sight.
The wolves had a ravenous appetite after days without prey.

meanings.definition: Unfaithfulness, especially the act of being unfaithful to a partner or cause.
examples: His infidelity ultimately destroyed the marriage.
The soldier was punished for his infidelity to the crown.

meanings.definition: To drive away or get rid of a feeling, belief, or condition.
examples: Her warm welcome quickly dispelled my nervousness.
The teacher tried to dispel several common myths about science.

meanings.definition: Strong disagreement or conflict within a group.
examples: Dissension among the members nearly split the committee.
The new policy caused great dissension in the office.

meanings.definition: Slightly sour, sharp, or biting, whether in taste or tone.
examples: The unripe apple had a subacid flavor.
She replied with a subacid remark that stung more than she intended.

meanings.definition: Something said or done to deceive or mislead; nonsense or trickery.
examples: He dismissed the salesman's claims as pure humbug.
The so-called miracle cure was nothing but humbug.

meanings.definition: The emission of a soft, glowing light without noticeable heat.
examples: The phosphorescence of the waves lit up the dark shoreline.
Certain deep-sea creatures produce a faint phosphorescence.

meanings.definition: The act of stopping or ceasing from an action.
examples: The court ordered the desistance of all logging in the protected forest.
His desistance from smoking improved his health within months.

meanings.definition: A ruler or person who holds great power or authority.
examples: The ancient potentate ruled over a vast empire.
Foreign potentates gathered for the grand coronation.

meanings.definition: Able to recover quickly from difficulty, illness, or hardship.
examples: Children are often remarkably resilient after setbacks.
The resilient community rebuilt its town after the flood.

meanings.definition: Able to spring back into shape after being bent or compressed.
examples: The resilient rubber returned to its original form once released.

meanings.definition: To move from one region or habitat to another, often seasonally.
examples: Many birds migrate south for the winter.
Thousands of workers migrated to the city in search of jobs.

meanings.definition: To push or thrust something down by force.
examples: The heavy boot detruded the stake deep into the wet ground.
Pressure from above detruded the layers of rock.

meanings.definition: A built-in bed or bunk on a ship, train, or other vehicle.
examples: She climbed into the upper berth of the sleeping car.
Each cabin on the ship had two berths.

meanings.definition: A place where a ship is moored or docked.
examples: The ferry eased slowly into its berth at the harbor.

meanings.definition: Unselfish concern for the welfare of others.
examples: Her altruism led her to volunteer every weekend at the shelter.
True altruism puts the needs of others before one's own.

meanings.definition: Shameless boldness or rude impudence.
examples: He had the effrontery to demand a refund after breaking the item himself.
She was shocked by the effrontery of his lie.

meanings.definition: To join or blend things together into a single whole, as by melting.
examples: The artist fused glass and metal into a single sculpture.
Their two styles fused to create something entirely new.

meanings.definition: Showing a lack of courage or determination; timid.
examples: His pusillanimous retreat at the first sign of trouble disappointed everyone.
Critics called the committee's pusillanimous decision a failure of nerve.

meanings.definition: To be greater in number, weight, force, or influence.
examples: The arguments in favor seemed to preponderate over the objections.
In this region, small farms preponderate over large estates.

meanings.definition: The state of being superior to or more distinguished than all others.
examples: The university earned preeminence in the field of medical research.
Her preeminence as a violinist was acknowledged worldwide.

meanings.definition: Showing the usual qualities or characteristics of a particular type or group.
examples: A typical school day starts at eight in the morning.
His calm reaction was typical of his easygoing nature.

meanings.definition: A lack or shortage of something necessary.
examples: A deficiency of vitamin C can cause serious health problems.
The report pointed out several deficiencies in the safety plan.

meanings.definition: To strike back or repay a wrong with a similar wrong.
examples: When the company cut prices, its rival retaliated by cutting its own.
He chose not to retaliate after the unfair insult.

meanings.definition: A published collection of selected writings by various authors.
examples: The anthology includes poems from a dozen different writers.
Her short story appeared in a famous anthology of science fiction.

meanings.definition: The part of a theater stage that lies in front of the curtain, framing the action.
examples: The actor stepped forward to the proscenium to deliver his final lines.
An ornate arch framed the proscenium of the old opera house.

meanings.definition: To solemnly give up or renounce something, often under oath.
examples: He vowed to forswear all violence from that day on.
She forswore sweets for the entire month.

meanings.definition: To ask for information about something.
examples: She stopped at the desk to inquire about train times.
He wrote to the company to inquire whether the job was still open.

meanings.definition: A small container, often of precious metal, used to hold the consecrated bread of the Eucharist.
examples: The priest carried the host to the sick in a small silver pyx.
The pyx was kept locked in the church's tabernacle.

meanings.definition: To add explanatory or critical notes to a text.
examples: The editor annotated the manuscript with helpful comments.
Students were asked to annotate the poem with their interpretations.

meanings.definition: A skillful sense of what to say or do to avoid giving offense.
examples: She handled the awkward situation with great tact.
It takes tact to deliver bad news kindly.

meanings.definition: To ride faster, farther, or better than another.
examples: The messenger managed to outride the pursuing soldiers.
Her sturdy horse could outride any other in the village.

meanings.definition: The spreading or scattering of something over a wide area.
examples: The diffusion of new ideas was sped up by the printing press.
Scientists studied the diffusion of the dye through the water.

meanings.definition: A reference work containing detailed information on many subjects or on one subject in depth.
examples: She looked up the country's history in the encyclopedia.
The online encyclopedia is updated by thousands of volunteers.

meanings.definition: An angel of the highest rank.
examples: According to tradition, the archangel Michael led the heavenly armies.
The painting showed a radiant archangel descending from the clouds.

meanings.definition: Seeming to have no end; endlessly long and tiresome.
examples: The interminable meeting dragged on past midnight.
We waited in an interminable line for tickets.

meanings.definition: The electrode through which electric current enters a device such as a battery or electrolytic cell.
examples: In the cell, electrons flow away from the anode.
The metal anode slowly corroded during the experiment.

meanings.definition: To assign too low a value or too little importance to something.
examples: Investors often undervalue companies that lack flashy products.
Don't undervalue the effort it took to finish the project.

meanings.definition: Dependent on or having confidence in someone or something.
examples: The town is heavily reliant on tourism for its income.
She is self-reliant and rarely asks others for help.

meanings.definition: To expose an area or object to smoke or fumes in order to disinfect it or kill pests.
examples: Workers had to fumigate the warehouse to get rid of the insects.
They fumigated the greenhouse before planting new seedlings.

meanings.definition: Having many curves and bends; winding.
examples: The sinuous river wound through the green valley.
A sinuous mountain road led to the village.

meanings.definition: In a way that is closest in space, time, or relationship; immediately.
examples: The fire was proximately caused by a frayed electrical wire.
The two events were proximately related, one leading directly to the other.

meanings.definition: To release or separate, especially from a yoke or a binding connection.
examples: The farmer unyoked the oxen at the end of the long day.
After years together, the partners decided to unyoke their businesses.

meanings.definition: A clear, deep understanding of a person, situation, or problem.
examples: Her years of experience gave her real insight into the issue.
The book offers valuable insight into how memory works.

meanings.definition: Calm and peaceful, with little disturbance.
examples: The placid lake reflected the mountains perfectly.
She remained placid even as the crowd grew loud.

meanings.definition: A formal warning or admonition.
examples: The bishop issued a monitory against the spreading rumors.
The old fable serves as a monitory to the greedy.

meanings.definition: Giving or serving as a warning.
examples: He raised a monitory finger before the children could run off.

meanings.definition: To break or go beyond a law, rule, or moral boundary.
examples: Anyone who transgresses the rules will be removed from the game.
He felt he had transgressed against his own principles.

meanings.definition: One of the small, flat polished surfaces cut into a gem.
examples: Each facet of the diamond caught the light beautifully.
The jeweler polished every facet of the stone.

meanings.definition: One aspect or side of something with many sides.
examples: We examined every facet of the proposal before deciding.

meanings.definition: A place or device that is dangerous to human life.
examples: The abandoned mine was a deadly man-trap of hidden shafts.
Old landowners once set a man-trap to catch trespassers.

meanings.definition: Having a large, fat body.
examples: The corpulent innkeeper laughed as he carried the heavy trays.
Years of rich food had made him quite corpulent.

meanings.definition: Able to be convinced or moved to act by argument, appeal, or emotion.
examples: The senator was persuadable on the issue once she saw the new data.
He's stubborn at first but ultimately persuadable.

meanings.definition: Likely to betray trust; disloyal or deceitful.
examples: His treacherous ally secretly passed information to the enemy.

meanings.definition: Presenting hidden dangers or hazards.
examples: The icy roads were treacherous after the storm.

meanings.definition: An etched stone surface used in engraving for printing.
examples: The printer prepared a lithotype to reproduce the intricate design.

meanings.definition: The fixed point on or against which a lever rests or pivots.
examples: By moving the fulcrum closer to the rock, she could lift it with less effort.

meanings.definition: A central element on which something else depends or turns.
examples: Trust was the fulcrum of their entire partnership.

meanings.definition: Tending to show feelings openly and strongly.
examples: She was warm and demonstrative, hugging everyone she met.

meanings.definition: Serving to point out or specify; (in grammar) indicating which thing is referred to.
examples: "This" and "those" are demonstrative pronouns.

meanings.definition: A medical examination of a dead body to determine the cause of death.
examples: The autopsy revealed that the victim had been poisoned.

meanings.definition: The act of caring for and encouraging the growth or development of someone or something.
examples: The garden flourished under her patient nurture.

meanings.definition: To care for and encourage the growth or development of.
examples: Good teachers nurture curiosity in their students.

meanings.definition: A departure from the main subject; a digression.
examples: His lecture was full of amusing divagations from the topic.

meanings.definition: To make something widely or publicly known.
examples: The tabloids blazoned the scandal across their front pages.

meanings.definition: To display or describe something conspicuously, especially heraldic arms.
examples: The family crest was blazoned on the gate.

meanings.definition: Hearty and enthusiastic enjoyment.
examples: She attacked the meal with gusto after the long hike.

meanings.definition: Of, relating to, or existing in the air.
examples: The drone captured stunning aerial views of the coastline.

meanings.definition: Light, delicate, or insubstantial as if belonging to the air.
examples: The dancer moved with an aerial grace.

meanings.definition: The measurement of the intensity or properties of light.
examples: Astronomers use photometry to gauge the brightness of distant stars.

meanings.definition: The quality or state of being icy, whether in temperature or manner.
examples: The iciness of the morning air made her shiver.
There was an unmistakable iciness in his reply.

meanings.definition: Once more, as if from the beginning; anew.
examples: After a good night's sleep, she tackled the problem afresh.

meanings.definition: The use of deception or dishonest schemes to achieve a goal.
examples: He won the contract through trickery rather than merit.

meanings.definition: An isolated hill with steep sides and a flat top, common in arid regions.
examples: The lone butte rose dramatically from the desert floor.

meanings.definition: An item of movable personal property.
examples: The furniture and livestock were listed among his chattels.

meanings.definition: Producing many offspring, fruit, or works.
examples: The prolific author published three novels in a single year.

meanings.definition: Present in large numbers or quantities; abundant.
examples: Wildflowers were prolific across the spring meadow.

meanings.definition: An animal, especially a dog, resulting from the crossing of different breeds.
examples: The shelter's friendliest dog was a scruffy mongrel.

meanings.definition: Not awake or aware of one's surroundings.
examples: The boxer lay unconscious on the canvas after the blow.

meanings.definition: Done or existing without one's conscious awareness.
examples: She had an unconscious habit of tapping her pen while thinking.

meanings.definition: A lively Spanish dance, or the music for it, typically accompanied by castanets and singing.
examples: The couple performed a passionate bolero at the festival.

meanings.definition: A short open jacket that ends above the waist.
examples: She wore a lace bolero over her sleeveless dress.

meanings.definition: Done secretly or stealthily to avoid notice.
examples: He took a surreptitious glance at his phone during the meeting.

meanings.definition: Coming at an unsuitable or inconvenient time.
examples: The phone rang at the most inopportune moment, just as he proposed.

meanings.definition: A sudden, violent upheaval or disaster, especially a great flood.
examples: The cataclysm swept away entire villages along the river.

meanings.definition: A momentous, often catastrophic event causing great change.
examples: The war was a cataclysm that reshaped the nation.

meanings.definition: To make someone anxious or unsettled; to disturb greatly.
examples: The strange noises in the attic perturbed the new tenants.

meanings.definition: To restore someone or something to a former position, state, or authority.
examples: After the appeal, the court reinstated her to her former job.

meanings.definition: Requiring much time, effort, or careful work.
examples: Translating the ancient manuscript was a laborious task.

meanings.definition: To continue firmly in a course of action despite opposition or difficulty.
examples: She persisted with her research even after repeated failures.

meanings.definition: To continue to exist or occur over a prolonged period.
examples: The fog persisted well into the afternoon.

meanings.definition: Operating below the threshold of conscious awareness.
examples: The advertisement contained subliminal messages designed to influence shoppers.

meanings.definition: To postpone or cancel the punishment of someone.
examples: The governor reprieved the prisoner hours before the sentence was to be carried out.

meanings.definition: A temporary relief from or delay of something difficult or unpleasant.
examples: The rain gave the firefighters a brief reprieve.
